head.lede: Two projects we're actively building, plus a sample of past client work. Twenty years' worth of code doesn't fit on one page — what's here is what we think is most relevant to the work we're focused on now.

active.label: 01 · Currently building
active.title: In active development.
active.lede: Internal projects we use to inform how we work — and one product we plan to ship.

image.alt: Foreman Run task workspace with a queue of in-flight runs, a code diff under review, and a structured plan of goal, steps, and verification

items.title: Foreman Run
items.description: An AI-agent harness with remote control. Custom workflows orchestrate work through an AI coding agent running locally or in a sandbox. We use it daily — it's part of why we can ship custom applications faster than firms our size usually can.

image.alt: Down Bad notification profiles dashboard listing uptime, quiet-hours digest, and dev sandbox profiles with their delivery channels

items.title: Down Bad
items.description: 24/7 uptime monitoring with more features than the established tools, at a flat $50 a year — not per monitor, not per site. Built because we wanted better monitoring for our own infrastructure than the market was offering.

past.label: 02 · Selected past work
past.title: Client engagements.
past.lede: A sample of what we've built for businesses over the years. Some are anonymized; in most cases the work was internal and not public-facing.

image.alt: rLinc AI Assistant analytics dashboard with question and user counts, success rate, response time, cost, feedback metrics, and usage and cost time-series charts

items.title: rLinc
items.description: An internal operations platform for managing clients, work, employee workflows, and accounting. Recently extended with an AI assistant — approved employees can ask natural-language questions against company data, with usage, cost, and feedback tracked through an admin analytics dashboard.

image.alt: Accent desktop app personal meeting screen with launch controls and conference, huddle, and break room cards

items.title: Accent
items.role: Unified communications · desktop and mobile app
items.description: An all-in-one business communications app: cloud phone, video meetings, team messaging, and third-party integrations brought into a single desktop and mobile experience. Built so teams can place calls, host conferences, and collaborate without bouncing between tools.

cta.sub: Tell us about it. A 30-minute call, no pitch, no pressure.
